## YouTube Algorithm: Updated Guide to How YouTube Algorithm Works in 2021

**Page type:** blog
**Summary:** A comprehensive guide explaining how the YouTube algorithm works in 2021, covering its evolution from view count to watch time to deep learning-based recommendations, and offering optimization tips for creators.

**Key facts:**
- YouTube has over 2 billion users and generates over 1 billion hours of watch time daily.
- Over 500 hours of content are uploaded to YouTube every minute.
- From 2005–2012, YouTube rewarded view count; from 2012–2015, it shifted to rewarding watch time.
- In 2016, Google engineers released a white paper describing a deep learning-based recommendation system using a 'candidate funnel' and a 'ranking funnel'.
- YouTube's Four Rs of responsibility: Remove harmful content, Raise up authoritative voices, Reward trusted creators, Reduce borderline content.
- Borderline content saw a 70% reduction in watch time following algorithm changes.
- In Q2 2019, YouTube removed over 100k videos, terminated 17k channels, and removed 500 million comments.
- 94% of top 10 search results for coronavirus topics came from high-authority channels, per YouTube CEO Susan Wojcicki.
- 81% of users occasionally watch algorithm-recommended videos; 15% do so regularly (Pew Research Centre).
- Over 200 million different videos appear on users' home feeds every day.
- The YouTube algorithm impacts discovery across 5 sections: Search, Suggested Videos, Trending, Home, and Subscriptions.
- Clickbait is identified by high click-through rate combined with very low watch time.
- View velocity—views gained in the first few hours after publication—is a key metric for the Subscriptions tab.

**Brand terminology:** real-time feedback loop; algorithm that follows audience; candidate funnel; ranking funnel; video corpus; watch time; view velocity; borderline content; Four Rs; deep learning; YouTube SERPs; audience retention; average session duration; click-through rate; YouTube discovery ads

**Audience:** YouTube video creators, marketers, and brands seeking to understand and optimize for the YouTube algorithm to increase views, watch time, and channel growth.

**Source:** https://www.igygrow.com/youtube-algorithm.html
